Argentina v Austria — 22 Jun, 10:30 PM IST (Group J)

Full group standings, scorers and the complete fixture list are on our FIFA World Cup 2026 page, updated through the day.
key_facts: Germany 2-1 Ivory Coast
Ecuador 0-0 Curaçao
Tunisia 0-4 Japan
Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia

stories.headline: Sloppy India Women lose Manchester thriller to South Africa, in danger of early T20 World Cup exit
stories.what: India Women suffered their first defeat of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, going down to South Africa in a thriller at Manchester. Marizanne Kapp was the standout performer for South Africa, whose disciplined bowling and fielding exposed a sloppy Indian batting display. The loss puts India's progress in the tournament in jeopardy, with the possibility of an early exit now a real concern.
stories.lens: A defeat to South Africa in a World Cup group stage match puts India Women under immediate pressure — one more loss could end their title hopes before the knockout rounds.
stories.article:
India Women crashed to a defeat against South Africa in a tense Women's T20 World Cup 2026 group-stage clash in Manchester, suffering their first loss of the tournament and raising serious questions about their campaign. The result leaves India in a precarious position in the standings, where another setback could effectively end their hopes of reaching the knockout stage.

South Africa's Marizanne Kapp was the architect of India's downfall, producing a brilliant performance that dismantled the Indian batting order at a critical juncture. Kapp's pace, accuracy and ability to take wickets at key moments proved too much for an Indian side that looked disjointed and indisciplined — described by multiple observers as sloppy, particularly in their shot selection and running between the wickets.

The defeat is a significant blow for a side that had opened the tournament with promise. India now face a must-win situation in their remaining group games, with the margin for error essentially gone. South Africa, on the other hand, have firmly established themselves as genuine contenders, their combination of big-hitting and disciplined bowling making them a formidable unit at this level.

For Indian fans, the result is a sharp reminder of how fine the margins are in T20 cricket. The team management and captain will need to regroup quickly — the squad has the talent to recover, but the schedule offers little breathing room. All eyes will be on India's next fixture as they look to arrest the slide and keep their World Cup alive.
key_facts: India Women lost to South Africa Women at Manchester
First defeat for India in the T20 World Cup 2026
Marizanne Kapp was the standout performer for South Africa
India now in danger of early tournament exit

stories.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes record-breaking 11-ball half-century for India A against Sri Lanka A
stories.what: Teenager Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced a stunning 11-ball half-century for India A against Sri Lanka A, setting a new record for the fastest fifty in the context of the match. The Rajasthan Royals prodigy, who shot to fame in the IPL, continued his extraordinary run of form with another breathtaking display of clean hitting. The innings reaffirmed his status as one of the most exciting young batting talents in Indian cricket.
stories.lens: Sooryavanshi's record-breaking knock signals that India may have unearthed a genuinely once-in-a-generation power-hitter — the kind who could reshape the country's T20 batting for the next decade.
stories.article:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi announced himself on the India A stage in the most emphatic way possible, blazing to a half-century off just 11 balls against Sri Lanka A — a record-breaking knock that left spectators and cricket analysts scrambling for comparisons. The teenager, who became a household name during IPL 2025 with Rajasthan Royals, showed that his fearless approach to batting translates beyond franchise cricket and onto the international representative stage.

Sooryavanshi's assault was characterised by clean striking, natural timing and a remarkable ability to pick the right ball to attack from the very first delivery he faced. Reaching fifty in 11 balls places his innings among the fastest half-centuries ever recorded in any format of the game, and it came against a Sri Lanka A attack that is far from ordinary.

For India's cricket ecosystem, the innings carries significant weight. The selectors and the national team management have been keeping a close watch on Sooryavanshi's progress, and a performance of this magnitude in an India A setting — one tier below full international cricket — makes it increasingly difficult to ignore his case for a senior call-up. His ability to accelerate from ball one is a skill set that T20 teams around the world pay a premium for.

Sooryavanshi is still in his mid-teens, making the record all the more staggering in its context. BBC Sport's coverage of the knock drew global attention, underlining that Indian cricket's next big thing may already be here — and in a hurry.
key_facts: Sooryavanshi scored a half-century off just 11 balls
Match: India A vs Sri Lanka A
Innings described as record-breaking in pace of fifty
Sooryavanshi is a Rajasthan Royals IPL prodigy

stories.headline: Cricket Ireland breaks silence on security threat to Sooryavanshi's potential India debut
stories.what: Cricket Ireland has publicly addressed reports of a security threat surrounding Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential debut for India, which is expected to come in the upcoming series in Ireland. The Irish board broke its silence to respond to concerns that had been swirling around the teenager's first senior international appearance. The situation highlights the heightened scrutiny and logistical complexity that now accompanies Sooryavanshi's every move given his extraordinary rise to fame.
stories.lens: A security threat around a young Indian debutant playing abroad is an unusual and serious development — it speaks to both Sooryavanshi's celebrity status and India's duty-of-care obligations towards players on tour.
stories.article:
Cricket Ireland issued a public statement addressing reported security concerns linked to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's anticipated debut for the Indian senior team, expected during India's tour of Ireland. The Irish board's decision to break its silence suggests the reports had reached a level of seriousness that required an official response, though the specific nature of the threat has not been confirmed in detail.

Sooryavanshi's meteoric rise — from domestic prodigy to IPL sensation and now India A match-winner — has made him one of cricket's most-watched teenagers globally. That level of visibility can attract unwanted attention, and concerns about player safety during overseas tours are taken seriously by both the BCCI and host boards. Cricket Ireland's statement is understood to have sought to reassure all parties that appropriate security arrangements are in place or under review.

The NDTV Sports report framed the development as a question mark over whether Sooryavanshi's debut could proceed as anticipated, though no official postponement or cancellation of the tour has been signalled. Sources suggest both boards remain in communication to ensure the visit goes ahead safely.

The episode underlines the extraordinary pressure that comes with being India's next big cricketing star. Sooryavanshi, still a teenager, is now navigating media scrutiny, selection debates, and security logistics simultaneously — a unique set of challenges that will test the administrative frameworks around him as much as it tests the player himself.
key_facts: Cricket Ireland addressed security concerns around Sooryavanshi's debut
Sooryavanshi's India debut expected during India's tour of Ireland
Nature of the security threat has not been officially detailed

stories.headline: Yashasvi Jaiswal Stars as India Complete Series Sweep
stories.what: Yashasvi Jaiswal led India to a series sweep, delivering a standout performance in the final match to seal a clean series win. The victory underlines India's dominant form with Jaiswal emerging as the decisive batting force across the series. It marks a commanding statement by the Indian side heading into their broader international schedule.
stories.lens: A series sweep confirms India's growing depth and Jaiswal's emergence as a match-winner — a sign of things to come for the national side.
stories.article:
India completed a series sweep with Yashasvi Jaiswal front and centre, the young opener delivering another commanding performance to seal a clean slate for the visiting side. The result caps a dominant run for India across the series, with Jaiswal's contributions at the top of the order proving decisive throughout.

Jaiswal, who has steadily cemented his place as one of India's most reliable batting weapons across formats, once again led from the front in the decisive match. His ability to anchor the innings while also rotating strike and accelerating at will has been the defining feature of India's batting during this series.

The sweep is a significant achievement for the Indian team, reflecting both individual brilliance and collective effort across the squad. For the management and selectors, it provides firm evidence of the team's consistency away from home conditions — always a key benchmark for a side with larger ambitions.

As reported by The New Indian Express, the series result will give the Indian camp considerable confidence ahead of upcoming fixtures. With Jaiswal in this form, India's top order looks increasingly settled, and the series win sends a clear message to future opponents.
key_facts: India complete a series sweep in their recent series
Yashasvi Jaiswal named as the standout performer
Jaiswal's batting was the decisive factor across the series

stories.headline: Sonam Khan breaks silence on 40-year-old rumour about casting in Tridev's iconic 'Oye Oye'
stories.what: Actor Sonam Khan has addressed a decades-old rumour surrounding the casting of the iconic song 'Oye Oye' from the 1989 Bollywood blockbuster Tridev. The song, featuring Sonam and choreographed memorably by Saroj Khan, became one of the defining pop-culture moments of late-1980s Hindi cinema. Khan's reaction sheds fresh light on backstage stories from a golden era of Bollywood, reigniting interest among fans of classic Hindi film music and nostalgia lovers.
stories.lens: For fans of 1980s Bollywood, Sonam Khan's firsthand account of the 'Oye Oye' casting story offers a rare, direct window into how one of Hindi cinema's most beloved songs came to be.
stories.article:
Nearly four decades after 'Oye Oye' became a nationwide sensation, Sonam Khan has finally responded to a persistent rumour about how she came to be cast in the iconic number from the 1989 blockbuster Tridev. The song, a high-energy dance track that helped define Bollywood's late-80s aesthetic, has remained a cultural touchstone — and the story behind its casting has circulated in film circles for years.

Khan's reaction, reported by The Indian Express, directly addresses a claim that has lingered since the film's release. While the specifics of the rumour involve the circumstances under which she was chosen for the number over others, Khan's firsthand account offers clarity — or at least her perspective — on an episode that has taken on an almost mythical quality in Bollywood lore.

Tridev, directed by Rajiv Rai and starring Sunny Deol, Jackie Shroff and Naseeruddin Shah, was one of the biggest hits of 1989. 'Oye Oye', with its memorable choreography by the late Saroj Khan and foot-tapping composition, became the film's most enduring legacy beyond its action sequences.

For a generation that grew up with the song, Sonam Khan's willingness to address the old rumour head-on is a welcome piece of Bollywood history — a reminder of the fascinating, often untold stories that lie behind cinema's most beloved moments.
key_facts: Sonam Khan addressed a 40-year-old casting rumour
Rumour concerns her role in 'Oye Oye' from 1989 film Tridev
'Oye Oye' is one of Hindi cinema's most iconic song sequences

stories.headline: 'My qaum are all Indians': Javed Akhtar claps back at troll, reaffirms his atheism
stories.what: Veteran lyricist and screenwriter Javed Akhtar has sharply responded to an online troll, declaring 'My qaum are all Indians' and reiterating that he is an atheist. The exchange drew wide attention on social media, with Akhtar refusing to be pigeonholed by religious or communal identity. The response underscores his long-standing public stance on secularism and national identity, resonating with many at a time of heightened social polarisation in India.
stories.lens: Akhtar's pointed rebuttal cuts to the heart of an ongoing online debate about identity and belonging in India, showing how public intellectuals are pushing back against communal trolling.
stories.article:
Javed Akhtar fired back at an online troll with characteristic precision this week, declaring 'My qaum are all Indians' and once again making clear that he considers himself an atheist — not a member of any religious community. The exchange, which surfaced on social media, quickly gained traction, with many users applauding the veteran lyricist for his directness.

Akhtar, one of Hindi cinema's most celebrated writers, has long been an outspoken advocate for secularism and rationalism. His response was not merely a personal rebuttal but a restatement of a worldview he has championed publicly for decades — that national identity supersedes religious or communal affiliation.

The incident reflects a broader pattern of public figures being targeted online with attempts to assign them a communal identity, often in inflammatory terms. Akhtar's refusal to accept that framing, and his insistence on a pan-Indian identity, struck a chord with a wide cross-section of followers.

The exchange is a reminder of the contested terrain of identity and belonging in contemporary India, where social media has amplified both communal provocation and the voices pushing back against it. For Akhtar, the message was simple and unambiguous: he belongs to no qaum other than India itself.
key_facts: Javed Akhtar responded to a social media troll
Declared 'My qaum are all Indians', reaffirmed atheism
Akhtar has been a longtime public advocate for secularism

stories.headline: World Music Day: Shankar Mahadevan says Indian musicians are 'doing wonders on the world stage'
stories.what: On World Music Day, celebrated annually on June 21, composer and singer Shankar Mahadevan spoke to Hindustan Times about the growing global footprint of Indian musicians. He said Indian artists are increasingly making their mark internationally, reflecting a broader surge in the global popularity of Indian music across genres — from classical and fusion to contemporary Bollywood-influenced sounds. Mahadevan's remarks highlight a cultural moment of genuine pride for the Indian music industry.
stories.lens: As Indian artists increasingly headline global stages and festivals, Mahadevan's assessment reflects a real shift in how Indian music is being received worldwide — something millions of music fans and aspiring musicians across India will find encouraging.
stories.article:
Shankar Mahadevan used World Music Day on June 21 to celebrate what he sees as a landmark era for Indian musicians, telling Hindustan Times that artists from India are 'doing wonders on the world stage.' The acclaimed composer and singer, himself a towering figure in Indian music, spoke with evident pride about the expanding global ambitions of his peers and the younger generation of Indian artists.

Mahadevan pointed to a growing trend of Indian musicians breaking into international markets and earning recognition well beyond the subcontinent. The shift is visible across genres — from classical and Hindustani traditions to contemporary fusion, film music and independent artists who have built substantial international audiences through streaming platforms and global tours.

World Music Day, observed across the world every June 21, is a moment when the industry pauses to reflect on music's cultural power. This year, conversations in India have centred on how the country's artists are no longer merely following global trends but helping to shape them — a sentiment Mahadevan's comments reinforced strongly.

For a country with one of the world's most diverse and prolific music cultures, the recognition on the global stage represents both an artistic and economic opportunity. Mahadevan's voice carries weight in this conversation, given his own career spanning Bollywood blockbusters, Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy collaborations, and a vocal style that has defined eras of Indian film music.
key_facts: Shankar Mahadevan spoke on World Music Day (June 21)
Said Indian musicians are 'doing wonders on the world stage'
Reflects growing global reach of Indian artists across genres

stories.headline: Johnny Lever surprises Zakir Khan on stage at Papa Yaar tour in Mumbai
stories.what: Legendary Bollywood comedian Johnny Lever made a surprise appearance on stage during comedian Zakir Khan's ongoing 'Papa Yaar' tour stop in Mumbai, delighting the audience with an unannounced cameo. Fans in attendance and online erupted with excitement, with many describing the moment as 'The genius with the GOAT', referencing Lever's iconic status in Indian comedy. The clip of the surprise appearance rapidly circulated on social media.
stories.lens: Two generations of Indian comedy — Lever's Bollywood era and Zakir Khan's stand-up generation — sharing a stage is a rare cultural moment that resonated deeply with fans across age groups.
stories.article:
Mumbai audiences at Zakir Khan's 'Papa Yaar' tour got an unexpected treat when Johnny Lever, one of Bollywood's most beloved comedians, walked on stage unannounced to join Khan mid-show. The surprise appearance sent the crowd into raptures and almost immediately went viral, with fans dubbing the moment a meeting of comedy royalty — 'The genius with the GOAT.'

Lever, whose physical comedy and timing made him a household name across three decades of Hindi cinema, and Khan, whose introspective, relatable stand-up style has built him a massive following among younger Indians, represent two distinct but celebrated chapters in the country's comedy tradition. Their on-stage chemistry, even briefly, became a talking point well beyond the venue.

Videos and photographs from the Mumbai show spread quickly across Instagram and X, with fans expressing joy at seeing the veteran comedian embrace the newer wave of Indian stand-up. For many, Lever's appearance was a validation of Khan's standing in the broader entertainment landscape.

The 'Papa Yaar' tour has been one of the notable stand-up events of 2025, and the Mumbai leg — now punctuated by Lever's cameo — is likely to be remembered as its most talked-about night. It also underlines how Indian comedy, once dominated by filmi slapstick, has evolved into a thriving live performance culture that can now pull icons from earlier eras onto its stage.
key_facts: Johnny Lever made a surprise on-stage appearance at Zakir Khan's show
The event was part of Khan's 'Papa Yaar' tour in Mumbai
Fans called the moment 'The genius with the GOAT' on social media

stories.headline: Priyanka Chopra to Star Alongside Angelina Jolie in New Hollywood Film
stories.what: Priyanka Chopra is set to share the screen with Angelina Jolie in an upcoming Hollywood production, marking another major step in her ongoing crossover career. The pairing of two globally recognised stars has generated significant buzz both in India and internationally. Details about the film's plot and release timeline have not yet been disclosed, but the casting itself signals a high-profile project. The announcement underscores Chopra's continued standing as one of the few Indian actors with sustained Hollywood reach.
stories.lens: For Indian audiences, seeing Priyanka Chopra headline a film alongside one of Hollywood's biggest names reinforces that Indian talent is genuinely competing — not just appearing — at the top of global cinema.
stories.article:
Priyanka Chopra is set to co-star with Angelina Jolie in a new Hollywood film, in what is shaping up to be one of the most high-profile cross-cultural castings in recent memory. The pairing brings together two of the entertainment world's most bankable international names, and the announcement has quickly become one of the most talked-about entertainment stories across Indian and global media.

Chopra, who first broke into Hollywood with ABC's Quantico and went on to appear in the Baywatch reboot and the Matrix Resurrections, has steadily built a credible presence in American cinema and television over the past decade. This project, however, represents a qualitative leap — sharing top billing with Jolie, an Oscar winner and one of Hollywood's most enduring A-listers, is a different proposition entirely.

While specific details about the film's genre, director, and release schedule remain under wraps, the casting alone signals that the production carries significant weight. Projects that bring together stars of this calibre typically attract major studio backing and wide international distribution, which would ensure the film reaches audiences well beyond the usual Bollywood diaspora circuit.

For India's entertainment industry, the development carries symbolic as much as commercial significance. Chopra has long been a template for Indian actors eyeing the global stage, and a genuine co-starring role alongside Jolie — rather than a supporting turn — is the kind of milestone that changes what the next generation of Indian film talent believes is possible.
key_facts: Priyanka Chopra to co-star with Angelina Jolie in a Hollywood film
Specific plot, director, and release date not yet disclosed
Chopra's most high-profile Hollywood pairing to date
